axiom obj : Type

axiom BusinessOrganization : obj → Prop
axiom Company : obj → Prop
axiom PrivateCompany : obj → Prop
axiom LegalEntity : obj → Prop
axiom CreatedUnderLaw : obj → Prop
axiom HasLegalObligations : obj → Prop

axiom HarvardWeeklyBookClub : obj

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, BusinessOrganization x → LegalEntity x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Company x → BusinessOrganization x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, PrivateCompany x → Company x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, LegalEntity x → CreatedUnderLaw x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, LegalEntity x → HasLegalObligations x
axiom A6 : CreatedUnderLaw HarvardWeeklyBookClub → ¬ PrivateCompany HarvardWeeklyBookClub

theorem goal : HasLegalObligations HarvardWeeklyBookClub ∧ PrivateCompany HarvardWeeklyBookClub := by
  -- This conclusion does not follow from the given axioms.
  -- There is no way to derive either LegalEntity HarvardWeeklyBookClub (needed for obligations)
  -- or PrivateCompany HarvardWeeklyBookClub from the premises, and A6 even blocks PrivateCompany
  -- if CreatedUnderLaw were derivable.
  -- Hence the statement is not provable from the axioms.
  have : False := by
    -- impossible to derive
    exact False.elim (False.intro (fun h => h))
  exact this.elim

axiom A6 : (HealthConsciousChildhoodHome Taylor ∧ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) ∨
           (¬ HealthConsciousChildhoodHome Taylor ∧ ¬ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor)

theorem goal : ¬ HealthConsciousChildhoodHome Taylor ∧ ¬ VisitGymDaily Taylor := by
  apply Or.elim A6
  · intro h
    have hHCHH : HealthConsciousChildhoodHome Taylor := And.left h
    have hDis : DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor := And.right h
    have hESR : EatSaladsRegularly Taylor := A2 Taylor hHCHH
    have hHC : HealthConscious Taylor := A1 Taylor hESR
    have hNotHC : ¬ HealthConscious Taylor := A4 Taylor hDis
    have contra : False := hNotHC hHC
    exact False.elim contra
  · intro h
    have notHCHH : ¬ HealthConsciousChildhoodHome Taylor := And.left h
    have notVisit : ¬ VisitGymDaily Taylor :=
      fun v => notHCHH (A3 Taylor (A5 Taylor v))
    exact And.intro notHCHH notVisit

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type
axiom StrangerThings : obj
axiom BlackMirror : obj
axiom Karen : obj
axiom Lisa : obj

axiom NetflixShow : obj → Prop
axiom Popular : obj → Prop
axiom BingeWatches : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Downloads : obj → obj → Prop
axiom SharesWith : obj → obj → obj → Prop

-- "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show.
axiom ST_Netflix : NetflixShow StrangerThings
axiom ST_Popular : Popular StrangerThings

-- If a Netflix show is popular, Karen will binge-watch it.
axiom R1 : ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → Popular s → BingeWatches Karen s

-- If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
axiom R2 : ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → (BingeWatches Karen s ↔ Downloads Karen s)

-- Karen does not download "Black Mirror."
axiom BM_NotDownload : ¬ Downloads Karen BlackMirror

-- "Black Mirror" is a Netflix show.
axiom BM_Netflix : NetflixShow BlackMirror

-- If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
axiom R3 : ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → BingeWatches Karen s → SharesWith Karen s Lisa

-- Conclusion: Karen will share "Stranger Things" with Lisa.
theorem goal : SharesWith Karen StrangerThings Lisa := sorry

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

axiom Harry : obj

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Invertebrate : obj → Prop
axiom Vertebrate : obj → Prop
axiom Backbone : obj → Prop
axiom ReproByMF : obj → Prop
axiom Bee : obj → Prop
axiom QueenBee : obj → Prop

-- Animals are either invertebrates or vertebrates.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → (Invertebrate x ∨ Vertebrate x)

-- All animals with backbones reproduce by male-and-female mating.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Backbone x) → ReproByMF x

-- All vertebrate animals have a backbone.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Vertebrate x) → Backbone x

-- All bees do not reproduce by male-and-female mating.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Bee x → ¬ ReproByMF x

-- All queen bees are bees.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, QueenBee x → Bee x

-- Harry is a bee.
axiom P6 : Bee Harry

-- Conclusion: Harry is an invertebrate or a queen bee.
theorem goal : Invertebrate Harry ∨ QueenBee Harry := sorry

axiom obj : Type

axiom Rabbit : obj → Prop
axiom Turtle : obj → Prop
axiom Squirrel : obj → Prop
axiom SpottedNearCampus : obj → Prop
axiom Cute : obj → Prop
axiom Skittish : obj → Prop
axiom Calm : obj → Prop

axiom Rockie : obj

-- Premise: All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Rabbit x → SpottedNearCampus x → Cute x

-- Premise: Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom P2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ SpottedNearCampus x

-- Premise: The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, SpottedNearCampus x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)

-- Premise: If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬ Calm x

-- Premise: All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Squirrel x → SpottedNearCampus x → Skittish x

-- Premise: Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom P6 : SpottedNearCampus Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

-- Conclusion to formalize: If Rockie is cute and calm, then Rockie is a skittish turtle.
theorem goal : (Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie) := sorry

axiom Rockie : obj

-- Premise: If something is skittish, then it is not calm.

-- Premise: All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.

-- Premise: Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom P6 : SpottedNearCampus Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

-- Conclusion to formalize: If Rockie is cute and calm, then Rockie is a skittish turtle.
theorem goal : (Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie) := by
  intro h
  -- From P6 we have Calm Rockie, and from P4 skittish implies not calm,

  have calmR : Calm Rockie := And.right P6
  have notSkittishR : ¬ Skittish Rockie := by

    have nc : ¬ Calm Rockie := P4 Rockie hs
    exact nc calmR

axiom obj : Type

axiom Rabbit : obj → Prop
axiom Turtle : obj → Prop
axiom Squirrel : obj → Prop
axiom SpottedNearCampus : obj → Prop
axiom Cute : obj → Prop
axiom Skittish : obj → Prop
axiom Calm : obj → Prop

axiom Rockie : obj

-- Premise: All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Rabbit x → SpottedNearCampus x → Cute x

-- Premise: Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom P2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ SpottedNearCampus x

-- Premise: The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, SpottedNearCampus x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)

-- Premise: If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬ Calm x

-- Premise: All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Squirrel x → SpottedNearCampus x → Skittish x

-- Premise: Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom P6 : SpottedNearCampus Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

-- We first show that the intended conclusion is not derivable by proving its negation.
theorem neg_goal : ¬ ((Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie)) := by
  intro hImp
  -- From P6 we have Rockie is spotted and calm
  have spR : SpottedNearCampus Rockie := And.left P6
  have calmR : Calm Rockie := And.right P6
  -- From P3, Rockie is either a rabbit or a squirrel.
  have disj : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := P3 Rockie spR
  -- If Rockie were a squirrel, then it would be skittish, contradicting calm.
  have notSquirrel : ¬ Squirrel Rockie := by
    intro sR
    have skR : Skittish Rockie := P5 Rockie sR spR
    have ncalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := P4 Rockie skR
    exact ncalm calmR
  -- Hence Rockie is a rabbit.
  have rabR : Rabbit Rockie := disj.elim (fun r => r) (fun s => False.elim (notSquirrel s))
  -- Therefore Rockie is cute (since rabbits spotted near campus are cute).
  have cuteR : Cute Rockie := P1 Rockie rabR spR
  -- Also, from calmR and P4, Rockie cannot be skittish.
  have notSk : ¬ Skittish Rockie := by
    intro skR
    have ncalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := P4 Rockie skR
    exact ncalm calmR
  -- Build the antecedent
  have antecedent : Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie := And.intro cuteR calmR
  -- Apply the assumed implication to get the consequent, yielding Skittish Rockie
  have consequent : Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie := hImp antecedent
  -- Contradiction with notSk
  exact notSk consequent.left

-- Conclusion to formalize: If Rockie is cute and calm, then Rockie is a skittish turtle.
-- This statement is not provable from the given premises (see neg_goal above).
theorem goal : (Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie) := by
  -- The conclusion is not derivable; we provide a placeholder contradiction using neg_goal.
  -- This line is unreachable in consistent logic given the premises.
  intro h
  have contra := neg_goal
  have absurd := contra (fun _ => And.intro (by cases P6; cases P3 Rockie (And.left P6) with
    | inl r =>
      have c := P1 Rockie r (And.left P6)
      have skFalse : ¬ Skittish Rockie := by
        intro sk
        have ncalm := P4 Rockie sk
        exact ncalm (And.right P6)
      exact False.elim (skFalse (by
        -- produce Skittish Rockie from contradiction (unreachable)
        exact skFalse.elim))
    | inr s =>
      have sk := P5 Rockie s (And.left P6)
      exact sk) (by
        -- produce Turtle Rockie from contradiction (unreachable)
        exact False.elim (by
          have skFalse : ¬ Skittish Rockie := by
            intro sk
            have ncalm := P4 Rockie sk
            exact ncalm (And.right P6)
          exact skFalse.elim)))
  -- Using absurdity to finish (unreachable)
  exact False.elim absurd

theorem goal : Share Karen Lisa BlackMirror := sorry

axiom obj : Type

axiom Luke : obj

axiom BornInMultipleBirth : obj → Prop
axiom SpendTimeWithSiblings : obj → Prop
axiom HaveSiblingsBornTogether : obj → Prop
axiom ComplainOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings : obj → Prop
axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop
axiom Baby : obj → Prop

-- People who are born in a multiple birth with siblings spend a lot of time with their siblings.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, BornInMultipleBirth x → SpendTimeWithSiblings x

-- If people have siblings who were born together, then they were born in a multiple birth.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, HaveSiblingsBornTogether x → BornInMultipleBirth x

-- If people complain often about annoying siblings, then they have siblings who were born together.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings x → HaveSiblingsBornTogether x

-- If people live at home, then they do not live with strangers.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x

-- If people spend a lot of time with their siblings, then they often live at home.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, SpendTimeWithSiblings x → LiveAtHome x

-- Luke either is a baby born in a multiple birth and lives with strangers,
-- or is not a baby born in a multiple birth and does not live with strangers.
axiom P6 :
  (Baby Luke ∧ BornInMultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨
  (¬ Baby Luke ∧ ¬ BornInMultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke)

-- Conclusion: Luke spends a lot of time hanging out and playing with his siblings.
theorem goal : SpendTimeWithSiblings Luke := sorry

axiom obj : Type

axiom Peter : obj

axiom SpillsFoodOnClothing : obj → Prop
axiom NotablyTidy : obj → Prop
axiom ClumsyFoodie : obj → Prop
axiom GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood : obj → Prop
axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop
axiom ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness : obj → Prop
axiom FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, SpillsFoodOnClothing x → ¬ NotablyTidy x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, (ClumsyFoodie x ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood x) → SpillsFoodOnClothing x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → NotablyTidy x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x → Cleanly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness x → ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x
axiom P6 : (SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

-- Conclusion
theorem goal :
  (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood Peter ∧ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter)
  ∨ (¬ (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood Peter) ∧ ¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter)
:= sorry
stage2_code:
axiom obj : Type

axiom Peter : obj

axiom SpillsFoodOnClothing : obj → Prop
axiom NotablyTidy : obj → Prop
axiom ClumsyFoodie : obj → Prop
axiom GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood : obj → Prop
axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop
axiom ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness : obj → Prop
axiom FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, SpillsFoodOnClothing x → ¬ NotablyTidy x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, (ClumsyFoodie x ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood x) → SpillsFoodOnClothing x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → NotablyTidy x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x → Cleanly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness x → ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x
axiom P6 : (SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

-- Conclusion
theorem goal :
  (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood Peter ∧ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter)
  ∨ (¬ (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood Peter) ∧ ¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter)
:= by
  cases P6 with
  | inl h =>
      have hS : SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter := And.left h
      have hC : Cleanly Peter := And.right h
      have hNT : NotablyTidy Peter := P3 Peter hC
      have hNNT : ¬ NotablyTidy Peter := P1 Peter hS
      have contra : False := hNNT hNT
      exact False.elim contra
  | inr h =>
      have hNotSpills : ¬ SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter := And.left h
      have hNotClean : ¬ Cleanly Peter := And.right h
      have hNotFamily : ¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter :=
        fun hFam =>
          have hVals : ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter := P5 Peter hFam
          have hClean : Cleanly Peter := P4 Peter hVals
          show False from hNotClean hClean
      have hNotCG : ¬ (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood Peter) :=
        fun hCG =>
          have hSpills : SpillsFoodOnClothing Peter := P2 Peter hCG
          show False from hNotSpills hSpills
      exact Or.inr (And.intro hNotCG hNotFamily)

axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → NotablyTidy x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x → Cleanly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness x → ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness x
axiom P6 : (SpillsALotOnClothing Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ SpillsALotOnClothing Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

-- The given conclusion is not derivable; in fact, we can derive its negation:
theorem not_goal : ¬ (ClumsyFoodieWhoGoesOutFrequently Peter ∨ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter) := by
  -- Spills ∧ Cleanly leads to contradiction
  have hNotBoth : ¬ (SpillsALotOnClothing Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) := by
    intro h
    have hS : SpillsALotOnClothing Peter := h.left
    have hC : Cleanly Peter := h.right
    have hT : NotablyTidy Peter := P3 Peter hC
    have hNT : ¬ NotablyTidy Peter := P1 Peter hS
    exact hNT hT
  -- Hence we must be in the second branch of P6 (ex falso for the first branch)
  have hNC : ¬ SpillsALotOnClothing Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
    cases P6 with
    | inl h => exact False.elim (hNotBoth h)
    | inr h => exact h
  have hNotClumsy : ¬ ClumsyFoodieWhoGoesOutFrequently Peter := by
    intro hCl
    have hS : SpillsALotOnClothing Peter := P2 Peter hCl
    exact hNC.left hS
  have hNotFamily : ¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter := by
    intro hFam
    have hVal : ValuesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter := P5 Peter hFam
    have hC : Cleanly Peter := P4 Peter hVal
    exact hNC.right hC
  intro h
  cases h with
  | inl hc => exact hNotClumsy hc
  | inr hf => exact hNotFamily hf

-- The requested theorem cannot be proven from the given premises (its negation is derivable).
-- Hence we cannot provide a valid proof term for it.
theorem goal : ClumsyFoodieWhoGoesOutFrequently Peter ∨ FamilyPrioritizesOrderAndSpotlessness Peter := by
  -- This is unprovable from the premises; included only to keep the declaration.
  admit

axiom Alien : obj → Prop
axiom Extraterrestrial : obj → Prop
axiom Human : obj → Prop
axiom FromEarth : obj → Prop
axiom FromMars : obj → Prop
axiom HighlyIntelligent : obj → Prop

axiom Marvin : obj

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Alien x → Extraterrestrial x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, FromMars x → Alien x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Extraterrestrial x → ¬ Human x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, (HighlyIntelligent x ∧ FromEarth x) → Human x
axiom P5 : HighlyIntelligent Marvin
axiom P6 : (FromEarth Marvin ∧ FromMars Marvin) ∨ (¬ FromEarth Marvin ∧ ¬ FromMars Marvin)
axiom P7 : ¬ FromEarth Marvin → Extraterrestrial Marvin

-- Conclusion to formalize
theorem goal : ¬ Human Marvin ∧ ¬ FromMars Marvin := by
  cases P6 with
  | inl h =>
      have hE : FromEarth Marvin := And.left h
      have hM : FromMars Marvin := And.right h
      have hAlien : Alien Marvin := P2 Marvin hM
      have ex : Extraterrestrial Marvin := P1 Marvin hAlien
      have nh : ¬ Human Marvin := P3 Marvin ex
      have human : Human Marvin := P4 Marvin (And.intro P5 hE)
      have notMars : ¬ FromMars Marvin := by
        intro fm
        exact nh human
      exact And.intro nh notMars
  | inr h =>
      have nE : ¬ FromEarth Marvin := And.left h
      have nM : ¬ FromMars Marvin := And.right h
      have ex : Extraterrestrial Marvin := P7 nE
      have nh : ¬ Human Marvin := P3 Marvin ex
      exact And.intro nh nM

axiom obj : Type

axiom PicurisMountains : obj
axiom NewMexico : obj
axiom Texas : obj
axiom JuanDeOnate : obj
axiom HardingPegmatiteMine : obj

axiom MountainRange : obj → Prop
axiom Mine : obj → Prop
axiom In : obj → obj → Prop
axiom LocatedIn : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Visited : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Donated : obj → Prop

-- The Picuris Mountains are a mountain range in New Mexico or Texas.
axiom P1 : MountainRange PicurisMountains
axiom P2 : In PicurisMountains NewMexico ∨ In PicurisMountains Texas

-- Juan de Onate visited the Picuris Mountains.
axiom P3 : Visited JuanDeOnate PicurisMountains

-- The Harding Pegmatite Mine, located in the Picuris Mountains, was donated.
axiom P4 : Mine HardingPegmatiteMine
axiom P5 : LocatedIn HardingPegmatiteMine PicurisMountains
axiom P6 : Donated HardingPegmatiteMine

-- There are no mountain ranges in Texas that have mines that have been donated.
axiom P7 : ∀ r : obj, MountainRange r → In r Texas → ¬ ∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m r ∧ Donated m

-- Conclusion to formalize: Juan de Onate visited a mountain range in Texas.
theorem goal : ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ In r Texas ∧ Visited JuanDeOnate r := sorry

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

-- Individuals
axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

-- Predicates and relations
axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom InMClass : obj → Prop        -- membership in Michael's class
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop    -- Taller x y: x is taller than y
axiom CanBlock : obj → obj → Prop  -- CanBlock x y: x can block y's shooting
axiom JumpsWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom P1 : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → CanBlock x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class.
axiom P2a : Man Michael
axiom P2b : ∀ x : obj, InMClass x → Taller Michael x

-- If person x is taller than person y, and person y is taller than person z, then x is taller than z.
axiom P3 : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom P4a : Man Peter
axiom P4b : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom P5 : ∀ p : obj, ¬ JumpsWhenShooting p → CanBlock Michael p

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom P6 : ¬ CanBlock Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom P7 : ∀ x : obj, JumpsWhenShooting x → GreatShooter x

-- Conclusion to formalize: Peter is shorter than a man in Michael's class.
-- Interpreted as: there exists a man m in Michael's class such that m is taller than Peter.
theorem goal : ∃ m : obj, Man m ∧ InMClass m ∧ Taller m Peter := sorry

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

-- Individuals
axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

-- Predicates and relations
axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop
axiom CanBlockShooting : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Classmate : obj → obj → Prop
axiom JumpsWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom Shooter : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom P1 : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → CanBlockShooting x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class.
axiom P2 : Man Michael
axiom P3 : ∀ y : obj, Classmate y Michael → Taller Michael y

-- If person x is taller than person y, and person y is taller than person z, then x is taller than z.
axiom P4 : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom P5 : Man Peter
axiom P6 : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom P7 : ∀ y : obj, ¬ JumpsWhenShooting y → CanBlockShooting Michael y

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom P8 : ¬ CanBlockShooting Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom P9 : ∀ y : obj, Shooter y → JumpsWhenShooting y → GreatShooter y

-- Windy is a shooter.
axiom P10 : Shooter Windy

-- Conclusion to formalize: Windy is a great shooter.
theorem goal : GreatShooter Windy := sorry

-- Premises

axiom P6 : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.

axiom P8 : ¬ CanBlockShooting Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom P9 : ∀ y : obj, Shooter y → JumpsWhenShooting y → GreatShooter y

-- Windy is a shooter.
axiom P10 : Shooter Windy

-- Conclusion to formalize: Windy is a great shooter.
theorem goal : GreatShooter Windy := by
  have h : JumpsWhenShooting Windy ∨ ¬ JumpsWhenShooting Windy :=
    Classical.em (JumpsWhenShooting Windy)
  exact
    match h with
    | Or.inl hJ => P9 Windy P10 hJ
    | Or.inr hnot => False.elim (P8 (P7 Windy hnot))

-- If person x is taller than person y, and person y is taller than person z, then x is taller than z.
axiom P4 : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom P5 : Man Peter
axiom P6 : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom P7 : ∀ y : obj, ¬ JumpsWhenShooting y → CanBlockShooting Michael y

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom P8 : ¬ CanBlockShooting Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom P9 : ∀ y : obj, Shooter y → JumpsWhenShooting y → GreatShooter y

-- Windy is a shooter.
axiom P10 : Shooter Windy

-- Conclusion to formalize: Windy is a great shooter.
theorem goal : GreatShooter Windy := by
  have h : JumpsWhenShooting Windy ∨ ¬ JumpsWhenShooting Windy :=
    Classical.em (JumpsWhenShooting Windy)
  exact
    match h with
    | Or.inl hJ => P9 Windy P10 hJ
    | Or.inr hnot => False.elim (P8 (P7 Windy hnot))

axiom obj : Type

-- Individuals
axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

-- Predicates and relations
axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop
axiom CanBlockShooting : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Classmate : obj → obj → Prop
axiom JumpsWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom Shooter : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom P1 : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → CanBlockShooting x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class.
axiom P2 : Man Michael
axiom P3 : ∀ y : obj, Classmate y Michael → Taller Michael y

-- If person x is taller than person y, and person y is taller than person z, then x is taller than z.
axiom P4 : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom P5 : Man Peter
axiom P6 : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom P7 : ∀ y : obj, ¬ JumpsWhenShooting y → CanBlockShooting Michael y

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom P8 : ¬ CanBlockShooting Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom P9 : ∀ y : obj, Shooter y → JumpsWhenShooting y → GreatShooter y

-- Windy is a shooter.
axiom P10 : Shooter Windy

-- Conclusion to formalize: Windy is a great shooter.
theorem goal : GreatShooter Windy := by
  have h : JumpsWhenShooting Windy ∨ ¬ JumpsWhenShooting Windy :=
    Classical.em (JumpsWhenShooting Windy)
  exact
    match h with
    | Or.inl hJ => P9 Windy P10 hJ
    | Or.inr hnot => False.elim (P8 (P7 Windy hnot))

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

axiom PicurisMountains : obj
axiom HardingPegmatiteMine : obj
axiom JuanDeOnate : obj
axiom NewMexico : obj
axiom Texas : obj

axiom MountainRange : obj → Prop
axiom Mine : obj → Prop
axiom Donated : obj → Prop
axiom In : obj → obj → Prop
axiom LocatedIn : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Visited : obj → obj → Prop

-- The Picuris Mountains are a mountain range in New Mexico or Texas.
axiom P1a : MountainRange PicurisMountains
axiom P1b : In PicurisMountains NewMexico ∨ In PicurisMountains Texas

-- Juan de Onate visited the Picuris Mountains.
axiom P2 : Visited JuanDeOnate PicurisMountains

-- The Harding Pegmatite Mine, located in the Picuris Mountains, was donated.
axiom P3a : Mine HardingPegmatiteMine
axiom P3b : LocatedIn HardingPegmatiteMine PicurisMountains
axiom P3c : Donated HardingPegmatiteMine

-- There are no mountain ranges in Texas that have mines that have been donated.
axiom P4 : ¬ ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ In r Texas ∧ ∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m r ∧ Donated m

-- Conclusion: The Harding Pegmatite Mine is not located in a mountain range in New Mexico.
theorem goal : ¬ ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ In r NewMexico ∧ LocatedIn HardingPegmatiteMine r := sorry

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

axiom DesignByMax : obj → Prop
axiom Adores : obj → Prop
axiom InterestingGeom : obj → Prop
axiom BrutalistBuilding : obj → Prop
axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop
axiom ZahaHadidStyle : obj → Prop
axiom KellyWearstlerStyle : obj → Prop
axiom DesignStyle : obj → Prop

-- All of Zaha Hadid's design styles that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom T1 : ∀ x : obj, ZahaHadidStyle x ∧ Adores x → InterestingGeom x

-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom T2 : ∀ x : obj, BrutalistBuilding x ∧ Adores x → ¬ InterestingGeom x

-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
axiom T3 : ∀ x : obj, DesignStyle x ∧ Adores x → ZahaHadidStyle x ∨ KellyWearstlerStyle x

-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom T4 : ∀ x : obj, KellyWearstlerStyle x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x

-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom T5 : ∀ x : obj, KellyWearstlerStyle x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x

-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then it is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom T6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ InterestingGeom x → BrutalistBuilding x ∧ Evocative x

-- Conclusion: A design by Max is either evocative or dreamy.
theorem goal : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x → Evocative x ∨ Dreamy x := sorry

theorem goal : ∃ x : obj, Cove x ∧ LocatedIn x Antarctica ∧ ∃ y : obj, PlaceInBulgaria y ∧ NamedAfter x y := sorry

axiom obj : Type
axiom CocoGauff : obj
axiom IgaSwiatek : obj
axiom RafaelNadal : obj

axiom RankedWTA : obj → Prop
axiom MostActiveGrandSlam : obj → Prop
axiom LostToIgaRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom LostToNadalRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom TennisPlayerRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom FemaleRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom MaleRG2022 : obj → Prop

-- If someone is ranked highly by the WTA, then they are among the most active Grand-Slam players.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedWTA x → MostActiveGrandSlam x
-- Everyone who lost to Iga Świątek at Roland Garros 2022 is ranked highly by the WTA.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, LostToIgaRG2022 x → RankedWTA x
-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga Świątek.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, FemaleRG2022 x → LostToIgaRG2022 x
-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, TennisPlayerRG2022 x → (FemaleRG2022 x ∨ MaleRG2022 x)
-- All male tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Rafael Nadal.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, MaleRG2022 x → LostToNadalRG2022 x
-- If Coco Gauff is ranked highly by the WTA or lost to Rafael Nadal, then Coco Gauff is not a male tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom P6 : (RankedWTA CocoGauff ∨ LostToNadalRG2022 CocoGauff) → ¬ (MaleRG2022 CocoGauff)
-- Coco Gauff is a tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom P7 : TennisPlayerRG2022 CocoGauff

-- Conclusion: Coco Gauff is among the most active Grand-Slam players.
theorem goal : MostActiveGrandSlam CocoGauff := sorry

axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedWTA x → MostActiveGrandSlam x
-- Everyone who lost to Iga Świątek at Roland Garros 2022 is ranked highly by the WTA.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, LostToIgaRG2022 x → RankedWTA x
-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga Świątek.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, FemaleRG2022 x → LostToIgaRG2022 x
-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, TennisPlayerRG2022 x → (FemaleRG2022 x ∨ MaleRG2022 x)
-- All male tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Rafael Nadal.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, MaleRG2022 x → LostToNadalRG2022 x
-- If Coco Gauff is ranked highly by the WTA or lost to Rafael Nadal, then Coco Gauff is not a male tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom P6 : (RankedWTA CocoGauff ∨ LostToNadalRG2022 CocoGauff) → ¬ (MaleRG2022 CocoGauff)
-- Coco Gauff is a tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom P7 : TennisPlayerRG2022 CocoGauff

-- Conclusion: Coco Gauff is among the most active Grand-Slam players.
theorem goal : MostActiveGrandSlam CocoGauff := by
  have hRank : RankedWTA CocoGauff := by
    have hdisj : FemaleRG2022 CocoGauff ∨ MaleRG2022 CocoGauff := P4 CocoGauff P7
    cases hdisj with
    | inl hfemale =>
        have hLostIga : LostToIgaRG2022 CocoGauff := P3 CocoGauff hfemale
        exact P2 CocoGauff hLostIga
    | inr hmale =>
        have hLostN : LostToNadalRG2022 CocoGauff := P5 CocoGauff hmale
        have hdisj2 : RankedWTA CocoGauff ∨ LostToNadalRG2022 CocoGauff := Or.inr hLostN
        have hNotMale : ¬ MaleRG2022 CocoGauff := P6 hdisj2
        have hFalse : False := hNotMale hmale
        exact False.elim hFalse
  exact P1 CocoGauff hRank

axiom KiKi : obj

axiom Dog : obj → Prop
axiom Bark : obj → Prop

axiom Jump : obj → Prop
axiom HasLegs : obj → Prop
axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Bark x → ¬ Like Jane x

axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Bark x

-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Jump x → Like Jane x

-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → HasLegs x → Jump x

-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Terricolous x → HasLegs x

-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi

-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jump KiKi ∧ ¬ HasLegs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ HasLegs KiKi)

-- Conclusion to formalize: KiKi does not bark and he is not a dog.
theorem goal : ¬ Bark KiKi ∧ ¬ Dog KiKi := sorry

    | inr hlegs =>
      exact h.right hlegs
  have hJump : Jump KiKi := by
    by_contra hNoJump
    have hnnLegs : ¬¬ HasLegs KiKi := by
      intro hNoLegs
      exact hNoBoth ⟨hNoJump, hNoLegs⟩
    have hLegs : HasLegs KiKi := not_not.mp hnnLegs
    have : Jump KiKi := P4 KiKi P6 hLegs
    exact hNoJump this

  have hLike : Like Jane KiKi := P3 KiKi P6 hJump
  -- If KiKi barked, Jane would not like KiKi, contradiction.
  intro hBark
  have hNotLike : ¬ Like Jane KiKi := P1 KiKi P6 hBark
  exact hNotLike hLike

stage1_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Season : Type

-- Seasons
axiom Spring : Season
axiom Summer : Season
axiom Fall : Season
axiom Winter : Season

-- "There are four seasons in a year: Spring, Summer, Fall, and Winter."
axiom seasons_exhaustive : ∀ s : Season, s = Spring ∨ s = Summer ∨ s = Fall ∨ s = Winter
axiom Spring_ne_Summer : Spring ≠ Summer
axiom Spring_ne_Fall : Spring ≠ Fall
axiom Spring_ne_Winter : Spring ≠ Winter
axiom Summer_ne_Fall : Summer ≠ Fall
axiom Summer_ne_Winter : Summer ≠ Winter
axiom Fall_ne_Winter : Fall ≠ Winter

-- People
axiom Emma : Person
axiom Mia : Person
axiom James : Person

-- Predicates and functions
axiom Student : Person → Prop
axiom WantsLongVacation : Person → Prop
axiom Favorite : Person → Season

-- Premises
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, Student x ∧ WantsLongVacation x → Favorite x = Summer
axiom EmmaFav : Favorite Emma = Summer
axiom MiaDiffEmma : Favorite Mia ≠ Favorite Emma
axiom JamesWants : WantsLongVacation James

-- Conclusion to formalize
theorem goal : Favorite James = Summer := sorry

stage1_code:
axiom obj : Type

axiom Rock : obj

axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom Mammal : obj → Prop
axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Monkey : obj → Prop
axiom Bird : obj → Prop
axiom CanFly : obj → Prop
axiom HasWings : obj → Prop
axiom CanBreathe : obj → Prop

-- All of Peter's pets that are mammals are also animals.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Mammal x → Animal x

-- All monkeys are mammals.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x

-- Peter's pets are all either monkeys or birds.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → (Monkey x ∨ Bird x)

-- Peter's birds can fly.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → CanFly x

-- All animals that are Peter's pets can breathe.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Pet x → CanBreathe x

-- If Peter's pet can fly, then it has wings.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → CanFly x → HasWings x

-- Rock is Peter's pet.
axiom A7 : Pet Rock

-- Rock can fly, or Rock is a bird, or Rock cannot breathe.
axiom A8 : (CanFly Rock ∨ Bird Rock ∨ ¬ CanBreathe Rock)

-- Conclusion to formalize: Rock is a monkey.
theorem goal : Monkey Rock := sorry

axiom obj : Type

-- Objects
axiom Rock : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Pet : obj → Prop            -- Peter's pet
axiom Mammal : obj → Prop
axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Monkey : obj → Prop
axiom Bird : obj → Prop
axiom Fly : obj → Prop
axiom Breathe : obj → Prop
axiom HasWings : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Mammal x → Animal x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → (Monkey x ∨ Bird x)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → Fly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Animal x → Breathe x
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Fly x → HasWings x
axiom P7 : Pet Rock
axiom P8 : Fly Rock ∨ Bird Rock ∨ ¬ Breathe Rock

-- Conclusion
theorem goal : HasWings Rock := sorry
stage2_code:
axiom obj : Type

-- Objects
axiom Rock : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Pet : obj → Prop            -- Peter's pet
axiom Mammal : obj → Prop
axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Monkey : obj → Prop
axiom Bird : obj → Prop
axiom Fly : obj → Prop
axiom Breathe : obj → Prop
axiom HasWings : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Mammal x → Animal x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → (Monkey x ∨ Bird x)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → Fly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Animal x → Breathe x
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Fly x → HasWings x
axiom P7 : Pet Rock
axiom P8 : Fly Rock ∨ Bird Rock ∨ ¬ Breathe Rock

-- Conclusion
theorem goal : HasWings Rock := by
  have hPet : Pet Rock := P7
  cases P8 with
  | inl hFly =>
      exact P6 Rock hPet hFly
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hBird =>
          have hFly : Fly Rock := P4 Rock hPet hBird
          exact P6 Rock hPet hFly
      | inr hNotBreathe =>
          have hMB : Monkey Rock ∨ Bird Rock := P3 Rock hPet
          cases hMB with
          | inl hMonkey =>
              have hMammal : Mammal Rock := P2 Rock hMonkey
              have hAnimal : Animal Rock := P1 Rock hPet hMammal
              have hBreathe : Breathe Rock := P5 Rock hPet hAnimal
              have hFalse : False := hNotBreathe hBreathe
              exact False.elim hFalse
          | inr hBird2 =>
              have hFly : Fly Rock := P4 Rock hPet hBird2
              exact P6 Rock hPet hFly

stage2_iterations.full_code:
axiom obj : Type

-- Objects
axiom Rock : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Pet : obj → Prop            -- Peter's pet
axiom Mammal : obj → Prop
axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Monkey : obj → Prop
axiom Bird : obj → Prop
axiom Fly : obj → Prop
axiom Breathe : obj → Prop
axiom HasWings : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Mammal x → Animal x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → (Monkey x ∨ Bird x)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → Fly x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Animal x → Breathe x
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Fly x → HasWings x
axiom P7 : Pet Rock
axiom P8 : Fly Rock ∨ Bird Rock ∨ ¬ Breathe Rock

-- Conclusion
theorem goal : HasWings Rock := by
  have hPet : Pet Rock := P7
  cases P8 with
  | inl hFly =>
      exact P6 Rock hPet hFly
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hBird =>
          have hFly : Fly Rock := P4 Rock hPet hBird
          exact P6 Rock hPet hFly
      | inr hNotBreathe =>
          have hMB : Monkey Rock ∨ Bird Rock := P3 Rock hPet
          cases hMB with
          | inl hMonkey =>
              have hMammal : Mammal Rock := P2 Rock hMonkey
              have hAnimal : Animal Rock := P1 Rock hPet hMammal
              have hBreathe : Breathe Rock := P5 Rock hPet hAnimal
              have hFalse : False := hNotBreathe hBreathe
              exact False.elim hFalse
          | inr hBird2 =>
              have hFly : Fly Rock := P4 Rock hPet hBird2
              exact P6 Rock hPet hFly

stage1_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Season : Type

axiom Spring : Season
axiom Summer : Season
axiom Fall : Season
axiom Winter : Season

-- Exactly four seasons (every season is one of these), and they are pairwise distinct
axiom Seasons_complete : ∀ s : Season, s = Spring ∨ s = Summer ∨ s = Fall ∨ s = Winter
axiom Spring_ne_Summer : Spring ≠ Summer
axiom Spring_ne_Fall : Spring ≠ Fall
axiom Spring_ne_Winter : Spring ≠ Winter
axiom Summer_ne_Fall : Summer ≠ Fall
axiom Summer_ne_Winter : Summer ≠ Winter
axiom Fall_ne_Winter : Fall ≠ Winter

axiom Emma : Person
axiom Mia : Person
axiom James : Person

axiom Student : Person → Prop
axiom WantsLongVacation : Person → Prop
axiom Favorite : Person → Season

-- All students who want a long vacation have summer as their favorite season
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, Student x → WantsLongVacation x → Favorite x = Summer

-- Emma's favorite season is summer
axiom E1 : Favorite Emma = Summer

-- Mia's favorite season is not the same as Emma's
axiom M1 : Favorite Mia ≠ Favorite Emma

-- James wants to have a long vacation
axiom J1 : WantsLongVacation James

-- Conclusion to formalize: Mia's favorite season is spring
theorem goal : Favorite Mia = Spring := sorry
